items.correct: Three sections: A, contrasting B, and a return to A (ABA).
items.why: Students assume 'three sections' means three different ones. Ternary has only TWO distinct ideas, but A returns - it's the return that makes it ternary, not the section count alone.

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: Whose 1967 Treatise is a famous graphic score?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cornelius Cardew. “John Cage” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was MIDI standardised?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1983. “1979” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Around what year did Les Paul pioneer multitrack recording?
items.why: Exact answer: 1947 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
